records.ety: [L. intumescere; pref. in- in + tumescere to swell up, incho. fr. tumere to swell. See Tumid.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Intumesced (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Intumescing (?).]

defs: To enlarge or expand with heat; to swell; specifically, to swell up or bubble up under the action of heat, as before the blowpipe.
quotes: In a higher heat, it intumesces, and melts into a yellowish black mass.
